confirmed.note: iter_plan batch 2: 14 *_iter functions repeat the same explicit-stack state machine (enum Phase + struct Frame{f,g,top_var,f_hi,g_hi,lo_result,phase} + stack.reserve(64) + while/switch ENTER/GOT_* scaffold) verbatim, swapping only terminal cases and the combine step; 15x identical stack.reserve(64) init block, 7x identical 'enum class Phase{ENTER,GOT_LO,GOT_HI}'. union vs intersec differ in only ~40 of 120 lines.

iter_plan batch 4: same manual-stack scaffold copied across the 7 count/profile/elmfreq/exactcount/countintersec _iter variants (7x stack.reserve block), each a partial edit of the prior with the per-op combine logic swapped. Same authored copypaste idiom as batch 2.

false_positives.what_happened: Pure monolith split: 2239 lines deleted from zdd_adv.cpp and re-created verbatim across 4 new per-category .cpp files (0 net logic change). A token/line dup detector run on the resulting tree (as the ca7523 hint did with zdd_adv_count.cpp:916-925 <- zdd_adv.cpp:850-859) reports the moved bodies as cross-file verbatim clones, but no code was duplicated - it was relocated. The original source no longer exists, so it is a move, not a copy.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: in cross-file mode, when scanning a commit diff treat a block as moved (not cloned) if the matching source lines are DELETED in the same commit (rename/move detection over hunks, like git -M). Net: if src-range total deletions ~= dst-range additions and source file shrinks correspondingly, suppress. Already partly addressed by 0beb2f7 rename detection; extend it to N-way splits (one source file -> many).

false_positives.what_happened: Header monolith split (mtbdd.h 2934 lines -> 3 internal headers + 18-line umbrella). Same move-not-copy situation as 9b365935 but for headers; verbatim template bodies relocated into new headers would be reported as cross-file clones.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: same move-detection over commit hunks (delete-in-source => suppress) plus exclude umbrella/re-export headers (file whose body is only #include directives) from being a clone endpoint.

cycle.desc: bdd_types.h -> bdd_class.h -> bdd_types.h  AND  bdd_types.h -> zdd_class.h -> bdd_types.h (3-node SCC)

intro_commits.edge: include/bdd_types.h:346-347 adds #include "bdd_class.h" and #include "zdd_class.h"; both new headers include "bdd_types.h" back (bdd_class.h:8, zdd_class.h:8)
intro_commits.reason: Proactive monolith split of bdd_types.h (1852 lines): BDD class -> bdd_class.h, ZDD class -> zdd_class.h. Author kept a trailing back-compat re-include of the two extracted class headers in bdd_types.h so existing #include "bdd_types.h" callers compile unchanged. The extracted headers still need the types/constants from bdd_types.h, so each includes it back -> 3-node include cycle. Classic split-with-back-compat-umbrella regression; single commit created the whole SCC.

cycle.evidence: Author code under include/ of graphillion/kyotodd (NOT vendored): bdd_types.h, bdd_class.h, zdd_class.h. SCC verified via Tarjan over basename include graph and via archcheck --graph (sccs_cyclic=1, largest_scc=3). Sibling mtbdd split (dbf24972) used an 18-line umbrella that does NOT include its children back, so it did not create a cycle - confirms b91602c's trailing re-include is the specific cause.
